content.full_text:
DUFFEL                    DUFFEL FINANCIAL AND CONSTRUCTION COMPANY                    FPPC
                                                                                                  MAY 29 11:18 AM '90

JOSEPH A. DUFFEL
PRESIDENT                                                                    May 25, 1990


Mr. Greg Baugher
Executive Director
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sixth Floor
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Baugher:

I am a member of the California Transportation Commission.  This
letter is to seek your advice regarding activities which would be
considered proper by the Fair Political Practices Commission
where I am aware that I have a conflict of interest.

My company owns 160 acres on Green Valley Road in Fairfield,
about one-half mile west of the intersection of 60 and 80.  A
new interchange is proposed at this intersection, but the City of
Fairfield has been trying for about three or four years to get
from Cal Trans the design of the interchange so that they can
proceed to construct the interchange with funds from a Mello-Roos
bond issue.

I recognize that it would be improper for me to vote on any
proposals which would allocate state money to this interchange
and I shall not.  However, to what extent is it proper for me to
work with the City of Fairfield and the State Department of
Transportation in an effort to get this project underway with
local funds?  I will, as a property owner, be paying into the
Assessment District.  Also, the interchange will greatly benefit
the state but the cost will be mostly borne by the locals.

Without your assistance, I would conclude that it is my job to
help get this project underway, as long as I do not vote on
spending any state money on a project which could benefit my
property.  This is in spite of the fact that my property will pay
its proper share as determined by the City of Fairfield for the
improvements through the Mello-Roos process.

I would appreciate an early answer.  Thank you very much.

                                              Yours very truly,

                                              Joseph A. Duffel
· TRADING
· DEVELOPMENT
· CONSTRUCTION

3730 MT. DIABLO BLVD     P.O. BOX 7003     LAFAYETTE, CA 94549     415/284-9600

sections.question: To what extent is it proper for me to work with the city of Fairfield and the State Department of Transportation in an effort to get this project underway with local funds? I will, as a property owner, be paying into the Assessment District. Also, the interchange will greatly benefit the state but the cost will be mostly borne by the locals.
sections.question_synthetic: A California Transportation Commission member who owns property that would benefit from a proposed interchange seeks advice on whether he may work with local officials to advance the project using local (Mello-Roos) funds, given that he will abstain from voting on any state funding allocations and will pay his share through the assessment district.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a private citizen to FPPC, not an FPPC advice letter response. No CONCLUSION or SHORT ANSWER section is present. Document dated May 25, 1990. Requestor seeks prospective advice on conflict of interest issues related to transportation infrastructure project.

I will, as a property owner, be paying into the Assessment District. Also, the interchange will greatly benefit the state but the cost will be mostly borne by the locals.

embedding.summary: Joseph Duffel, a California Transportation Commission member and property owner, requests FPPC guidance on the propriety of assisting the City of Fairfield and Caltrans in advancing a proposed interchange project using local Mello-Roos bond funds rather than state money, while noting his conflict of interest and commitment to abstain from voting on state allocations.
